A statue at the Will Rogers Memorial bears this epitaph, his most famous line
clues.response: \"I never met a man I didn't like\"

clues.clue: The epitaph on his footstone reads, "Truth & history. 21 men. The boy bandit king — he died as he lived.\"
This New Englander's "I had a lover's quarrel with the world" is from his poem "The Lesson For Today\"
His tombstone says, "He gave to man control over that dreadful scourge, yellow fever\"
Mount Likavitos, not the Acropolis, is the highest point in this city
You'll find a Great St. Bernard & a Little St. Bernard Pass in this European mountain system
In 1875, a Scot, Geo. Anderson, became the 1st person to climb Half-Dome in this U.S. park
Unlike nearby Everest, 22,958' Machapuchare has never been climbed because this country forbids it
The 1988 Winter Olympics took place in the foothills of these mountains
Legend says the missionaries 1st covered the near-naked native women with these long, loose-fitting dresses
80% of the residents live on this island, Hawaii's only metropolitan area
Name of the college all-star football game held annually in Hawaii
This senator from Hawaii chaired the Iran-Contra hearings
Father Damien founded his leper colony on this island
The logo of this state's turnpike, a stretch of I-90, once featured Paul Revere; now pilgrim hats are depicted
This road in London's Notting Hill claims the world's largest market selling antiques—sadly, not mushrooms
This city's Las Ramblas is a favorite meeting place for young Catalonians & a mecca for street artists
The USA's first federally funded road, the 600-mile National Road reached this current state capital at about its halfway point
A 4,600-year-old Egyptian road is the world's oldest paved road; it was used to transport building materials to this pyramid site
On September 20, 1904 he flew the first complete circle by an airplane while Orville watched
In 1968 this German airline was the first to put the Boeing 737 into service
In 1952 this country's de Havilland Comet became the 1st jet plane in commercial service
In 1933 he became the first man to fly solo around the world; 2 years later he died in a crash in Alaska
The first airship to make a round trip crossing of the Atlantic was one of these in 1919
What "SWAK" on a letter signifies
A snafu is short for this state of confusion
What the North American "DEW" line is short for
You can't get a shot at this "BAR", but you can shoot with it
The 2 countries whose soldiers are called ANZACs
This show about the bionic Steve Austin was based on the novel "Cyborg\"
Michael Sheen & David Tennant play an angel & demon in this series based on a Neil Gaiman-Terry Pratchett comic novel
Played by Timothy Olyphant on "Justified", Raylan Givens appeared earlier in the short story "Fire in the Hole" by this author
Season 5 of this detective show was largely based on Michael Connelly's "Two Kinds of Truth\"
Based on a Caroline Kepnes novel, this Lifetime/Netflix series about stalking is one creepy pronoun
If you neeze, a variant of this word, you may be blessed
Candied meant frozen, so discandy, a verb, meant this
Term for a fool's stick with a fool's head on top; it's now a trinket like bangles & beads
The upshot was the final shot or conclusion of a contest in this sport
It's what an anthropophaginian is
The 2 things created in the 1st line of the King James Bible
\"You don't know about me without you have read...The Adventures of Tom Sawyer\"
1st word in this Orson Welles movie is also the title character's last
\"It was love at first sight" between Yossarian & his chaplain in this novel
It is "the cruelest month" in T.S. Eliot's "The Waste Land\"
The Biblioteca Nacional Venezuela is in this city
This Slovakian capital with "slav" in its name was occupied by the Slavs in the 8th century
One of the 2 world capitals on the Rio de la Plata estuary
This capital of Bangladesh is just west of the Meghna River
This capital of Ghana is on the Gulf of Guinea
Azuma, Krakatoa
Santorini, Stromboli
Nyiragongo, Fogo
Deception Island, Erebus
Kiska, Popocatepetl

Larger than life on the courts, this title Bull went "To The Max" (about 8 stories high) in 2000
\"The Magic Box" follows these 2 illusionists from war-torn Germany to Las Vegas, & all in 3-D
A 1999 film explores the effect this weather phenomenon had on "The Island of the Sharks\"
Astronauts became cinematographers for the 1997 film "Mission to" this space station
There's a University of Georgia campus in this city that shares its name with a European capital
Flags honoring the nations who have hosted this surround the fountain in Atlanta's Centennial Park
Have a cookie in Savannah & visit the birthplace of Juliette Gordon Low, who founded this group in 1912
In Warwick, a festival honors this treat, Georgia's official prepared food
At this Georgia fort, an institute called WHINSEC is the successor to the controversial School of the Americas
Corkscrew, nail file, sharp blade, iconic red handle
Frame, sash, glass
Minaret, mihrab, ablutions area
A handheld item: canopy, shaft, springs, handle
The tumba, made of a gourd, about 20 parda or frets, a 3-foot-long neck, main & sympathetic strings
The archbishop of this district is the primate of all England
China has a "grand" one, & so does Venice
John Steinbeck described this district of Monterey as "a poem, a stink, a grating noise\"
Spanning 1,800 feet, the Quebec Bridge is the world's longest bridge of this type
\"Optimism" is the alternate title of this Voltaire work
It was Charlie Sheen's original last name; his older brother still uses it
Born Stefania Federkiewicz, she used the name Taffy Paul before she became a TV "Hart"throb
This "Die Hard" co-star's real name is Bonnie Culkin; Macaulay is her nephew
This comic actor-director's "Mother" could tell you his real name is Albert Einstein
It's Jason Patric's real last name; his father was a movie "Exorcist\"

The Pacific species of this animal can have an arm span more than 20 feet long

Magnetite is an important one of iron
Lutzelburg is an old name for the capital of this grand duchy
After entering Holland, this river divides into 3 branches: the Waal, the Lek & the Ijssel
Although British Columbia is Canada's only province on the Pacific, this territory is farther west
This French island in the Mediterranean was once ruled by the Bank of San Giorgio in Genoa
Finland's northernmost point lies at its border with this country
The Goliath birdeater, a South American type of this spider, makes a hissing sound when threatened
The leaves of a small shrub called the Egyptian privet produce this orange-brown dye used to color hair
The name of this water bird comes from the Latin Corvus marinus, which means "sea raven\"
Hard to find in its natural habitat, the Ituri Rainforest, this smaller giraffe relative was unknown to Western science until 1900
The Indian pipe plant is mycoheterotrophic, meaning it gets most of its food directly from this type of organism
The Yakima: to this larger Pacific Northwest river
The Yangtze: a sea with this country in its name

The Hudson: "Upper" this Bay
The Vistula: this sea
Seldom leaving the ocean, sea otters sleep & eat in this position
A member of the herring family, it's named for the Italian island in he Mediterranean where it was first caught
The world's largest herd of the fur type of these makes its home on Alaska's Pribilof Islands
Scientifically known as Orcinus orca, it may reach a length of nearly 30 feet
This spiny-skinned sea animal of the class asteroidea drops off arms as a defensive measure & then grows new ones
The name of this dynasty of 1368 to 1644 often comes between "priceless" & "vase\"
A samovar is a large urn traditionally used to make this
The Grecian urn Keats admired displayed a youth about to do this to a maiden
This American designer developed the opulent Favrile glass for his Art Nouveau vases
This vase in the British Museum was broken in 1845, repaired and then re-repaired in 1988
Cheers to Henry IV, who became France's first king from this ruling house in 1589

Named for a war minister, this 1930s "Line" of forts & small concrete bunkers, built to stop the Germans, didn't
After WWI, Germany lost this hyphenated border region to France, got it back in WWII, then lost it to France again
The first examples of art by prehistoric people are Cro-Magnon, like at this French cave discovered by 4 teens in 1940
Based on a Stephen King novel, this '88 musical featured a high school dance to remember but closed after only 5 performances
Shuttered after only 6 shows, "Into the Light" is about a physicist seeking the truth about this fabled burial cloth of Jesus
A sequel to this musical, "Bring Back Birdie" closed after 4 shows but still earned Chita Rivera a Tony nomination
\"Bobbi Boland", meant to be a star vehicle for this late Charlie's Angel, never opened, closing after 7 previews
\"La Strada", based on this director's film, starred a young Bernadette Peters & said arrivederci after one show
In 1826 the speed of sound in water was first calculated in this largest Alpine lake
This river springs from 2 small streams in the Black Forest, the Breg & the Brigach
The bonnie, bonnie banks of this largest lake in Great Britain lie just 14 miles from Glasgow
